entry.title: Introduction - Vikas Bhadani (SAP Solution Extensions through Icertis)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m excited to be part of the SAP Community and looking forward to engaging with this network of practitioners and experts.

I bring experience across SAP MM, SAP Ariba, and S/4HANA, with a strong focus on end-to-end Source-to-Pay processes. Over the years, I’ve worked extensively in the Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) space, helping organizations align sourcing, contracting, and procurement execution for better business outcomes.

Currently, I’m closely involved with SAP Ariba Contract Intelligence by Icertis, collaborating with customers and partners to design SAP Solution Extension offerings. My work primarily revolves around architecting solution designs, shaping value propositions, and enabling scalable, integrated CLM solutions within the SAP ecosystem.

I’m particularly interested in discussions around Source-to-Contract alignment, CLM innovations, and SAP–Icertis integrations. Looking forward to learning from this community and contributing wherever I can.

Thanks,
Vikas Bhadani

entry.title: Introduction to Next-Gen SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Introduction

Next-gen SAP Ariba marks a foundational transformation of SAP's procurement platform. It is the first modern, AI-native source-to-pay suite designed for the era of intelligent business operations. This comprehensive modernization, launched with the February 2026 (2602) release, transforms procurement from reactive to proactive, manual to autonomous, and siloed to orchestrated across the entire business ecosystem.

IntroductionNext-gen SAP Ariba is built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) using a modern, cloud-native architecture with API-first integration patterns. For a brief overview of the solution, kindly refer this page - Next-Gen SAP Ariba | Smart AI Source-to-Pay Software. For the quick demo, kindly refer this video (8 minutes).

Next-Gen SAP AribaFigure below depicts the capabilities & business impact of Next-gen SAP Ariba.Next-gen SAP Ariba - Capabilities & Business Impact

 

Next-Gen SAP Ariba - Solution Overview

Next-gen SAP Ariba consists of 6 major solution areas - Category Management, Suppliers, Sourcing, Contract Management, Buying, Invoicing.Next-gen SAP Ariba - 6 Solution AreasKey Features

  • Advanced Sourcing Capabilities: AI-driven sourcing engine with demand aggregation, bid analysis, scenario modeling, and enhanced supplier collaboration tools
  • Intelligent Contract Management: Features AI-powered contract authoring, automatic contract summaries using SAP AI Core services, and integrated Icertis Contract Intelligence capabilities
  • Enhanced Supplier Management: Unified supplier data model across all modules with AI-generated supplier summaries, performance scorecards, and comprehensive risk assessment
  • Unified Source-to-Pay Suite: Comprehensive platform covering sourcing, contracts, supplier management, buying, and invoicing with seamless data flow and process integration
  • AI-Native Architecture: Built on SAP Business Technology Platform with embedded Joule AI assistants providing operational workflow support, supplier discovery, and automated contract summarization

Figure below depicts the Next-gen SAP Ariba's Architecture & Integration FoundationNext-gen SAP Ariba - Architecture & Integration

Comparison: Next-gen SAP Ariba vs Current SAP Ariba

Next-gen SAP Ariba is not an upgrade — it’s a complete reinvention into an AI-powered, unified, and cloud-native procurement platform built for end-to-end orchestration.

Following figure shows the comparison.Comparison - Current SAP Ariba Vs Next-gen SAP Ariba

 

Next-Gen SAP Ariba Solutions

Next-gen SAP Ariba consists of 6 major solution areas - Category Management, Suppliers, Sourcing, Contract Management, Buying, Invoicing.

SAP Ariba Launchpad (SAL) - This welcome screen acts like the Central Hub for Procurement. It is the new central access point for all SAP Ariba applications providing consistent user experience. The launchpad is designed to be highly personal and relevant to the user. It dynamically renders content based on specific roles and current processes. This means a casual buyer and a sourcing manager will see different, tailored views.

Apart from the 6 major solution areas mentioned above, SAL also covers Analytics & Administration Center menus. For more details, kindly refer these links.

  • Next-Gen SAP Ariba | SAP Help Portal |
  • SAP Ariba Solutions Overview | SAP Help Portal
  • Getting Started with SAP Ariba Solutions | SAP Help Portal
  • View Capabilities for End-Users in SAP Ariba Solutions | SAP Help Portal

SAP Ariba Launchpad (SAL)

Sourcing

The transition to Next-gen SAP Ariba Sourcing represents a fundamental move from manual, template-heavy workflows to an autonomous, AI-orchestrated environment. This shift is primarily driven by  its foundation on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

Also, Next-gen SAP Ariba Sourcing introduces native Purchase Requisition (PR) integration, eliminating the mandatory dependency on SAP ERP RFQ documents. Sourcing can now be initiated directly from PRs, with RFQs becoming optional rather than required. It enables aggregation of multiple PRs into a single sourcing event, improving leverage and efficiency. A new demand aggregation engine applies business rules to decide what to group, when to source, and which suppliers to invite. Overall, this marks a shift from RFQ-driven integration to PR-driven, orchestrated sourcing, making the process more flexible, scalable, and intelligent.

Sourcing Comparison - Current SAP Ariba Vs Next-gen SAP Ariba

Sourcing - Aggregation Workbench

Sourcing - AI Summary

Sourcing - Auto Scenarios

Contracts

Next-gen Contract Management transforms Ariba from a document repository into an AI-powered, fully integrated contract intelligence platform driving better decisions, compliance, and speed.

Contracts Comparison - Current SAP Ariba Vs Next-gen SAP Ariba

Contract Details

Contract - Terms & Items

Contract - Stakeholders & Signatures

Supplier Management

Supplier Management in next-gen SAP Ariba moves from a fragmented module to a unified architectural spine built on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). 

Highlights

  • Unified Supplier Master: All modules (Sourcing, Contracts, Buying, and Invoicing) now share a single "source of truth," eliminating the need for reconciliation across legacy siloed records. 
  • AI-Driven Insights: Embedded AI via Joule provides instant, AI-generated supplier summaries and performance trends, moving governance from manual reporting to native platform discipline. 
  • Enhanced 360 Profiles: Reimagined supplier profiles offer a holistic view of risk analysis and sustainability tracking (Green Ledger) to help build more resilient partnerships. 
  • Centralized Governance: The platform features a single entry point for supplier actions (CRUD), ensuring multi-address synchronization and cross-module contact replication.

Supplier List

Supplier Details

Supplier Evaluation

Supplier Evaluation2

Category Management

Category Management and SAP Ariba Invoicing (earlier SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management, CIM) were the pioneering applications in the procurement portfolio to be developed natively on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). As there were already on BTP, their user interface was already aligned with the modern SAP Fiori (Horizon theme) design language that the rest of the SAP Ariba suite is now adopting through Next-gen SAP Ariba. So, the look and feel for these 2 solutions remains largely the same with exception of new enhancements. For more details on Category Management, kindly refer this post - Category Management with SAP Ariba

Category Management - Category Profile

Category Management - Strategy and Plan

Category Management - Strategy Execution

Buying

Next-gen SAP Ariba Buying is an AI-native solution built on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) that provides a modernized, consumer-grade purchasing experience. It utilizes SAP Joule, an integrated AI-copilot, to assist users with routine tasks and guide them through complex operational workflows. The platform features guided buying to enforce company policies through proactive warnings and automated validation, significantly enhancing organizational compliance.

Buying - My Requests

Request Details

Invoicing

SAP Ariba Invoicing (earlier SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management, CIM) is now the invoicing solution in Next-gen SAP Ariba replacing the classic SAP Ariba Invoice Management. For more details on the solution, kindly refer this post - Managing Invoices with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

Invoice Details

Analytics

The Analytics section in the SAP Ariba Launchpad (SAL) is based on the Spend Analysis Layer and the SAP Spend Control Tower, marking a shift from static historical reporting to proactive, AI-driven intelligence.

Highlights:

  • Real-Time Visibility: Integration with the SAP Spend Control Tower provides organizations with immediate visibility into global spend patterns.
  • AI-Powered Classification: The system leverages AI to automatically classify spend categories, helping identify cost-saving opportunities and "leakage" more accurately.
  • Unified Data Foundation: Built on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), analytics now pull from a single source of truth, ensuring consistency across sourcing, contracts, and buying modules.
  • Predictive Insights: Rather than just reviewing past performance, the platform uses embedded AI to provide predictive alerts regarding supplier risk and market trends.
  • Personalized Dashboards: Through the SAP Ariba Launchpad, users can access tailored dashboard cards that prioritize urgent tasks and monitor key operational KPIs in real-time.

Analytics - Dashboards

 

Next-gen SAP Business Network

Next-gen SAP Business Network is SAP’s evolution of the world’s largest business commerce network into an AI-native, cloud-first collaboration platform. Built on SAP Business Technology Platform, it creates a unified ecosystem where buyers, suppliers, and partners connect, collaborate, and transact seamlessly across the source-to-pay lifecycle. The platform transforms traditional network interactions into intelligent, data-driven, and real-time business engagement at scale.

Kindly refer these 2 links for collection of short videos highlighting the changes in Next-gen SAP Ariba Business Network for Buyers and Suppliers.

  • Buyer Network
  • Supplier Network

Graphics below summarizes the capabilities, architecture & business impact, and migration aspects of Next-gen SAP Business Network.

Next-gen SBN - Capabilities

Next-gen SBN - Architecture & Business Impact

Next-gen SBN - Transition

 

Next-Gen SAP Ariba - System Setup & Administration

For details on system setup and administration, kindly refer these links.

  • Getting Your System Ready | SAP Help Portal
  • Administration Center for SAP Ariba Solutions | SAP Help Portal
  • APIs for Next-Gen SAP Ariba Solutions | SAP Help Portal
  • Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Administering Next-Gen SAP Integration Suite, Managed Gateway For Spend Management and SAP Business Network | SAP Help Portal

Next-gen SAP Ariba - Administration Center

 

Other Related Topics

Partner Enablement

Partner enablement for Next-gen SAP Ariba is delivered through a structured ecosystem of learning journeys, hands-on training, implementation tools, and continuous support—designed to move partners from knowledge to real-world execution quickly. A dedicated page on Partner Portal has all relevant resources on the topic consolidated for easy access - SAP Partner Portal | Next-gen SAP Ariba.

Learning Courses

  • Positioning next-gen SAP Ariba (1 Hour)
  • Transforming Procurement: Introduction to Next-gen SAP Ariba for Partners (Recording - 1 Hour)
  • Implementing Next-Gen SAP Ariba: Onboarding through Extensibility (Recording - 3 Hours)
  • Selling Next-Gen SAP Ariba: Guidance for Partners (Learning Hub)(By Mark Pickering, Dan, Prem)
  • Selling Next-Gen SAP Ariba: Guidance for Partners (Recording - 1 Hour)

Test, demo, and development (TDD)

SKU 8007927 represents the Cloud test, demo, and development (TDD) for SAP Ariba solutions. This SKU is designed for SAP partners to build, test, and demonstrate functionalities within SAP Ariba applications in a non-productive, cloud-based environment. This TDD SKU has now been updated with the Next-gen fulfillment profile and is now ready for subscription. Partners can purchase the SKU 8007927, which includes the Next-gen entitlements. For any query, please email tdd@sap.com.

For details on TDD licenses and subscription process, kindly refer SAP Partner Portal | Test, demo, and development: Subscription. For pricing details, kindly refer SAP Partner Portal | Cloud Test and Demo: Significantly Reduced Price for Core Cloud Solutions.

Typical 'SAP for Me' screen with new TDD entitlements

For help regarding onboarding, kindly refer these two resources:

  • Onboarding Resource Center - Spend Management - It has related SAP resources, step-by-step guides, assets, webcast sessions links, all at one place.
  • Road Map Viewer: SAP Activate for SAP Ariba - The roadmap is intended to guide the implementation team through a SAP Ariba solution implementation and deployment. It is comprised of Project Phases, Deliverables and Tasks in accordance with the SAP Activate methodology and structure, which have been listed in sequential order based on activity start and can be easily adjusted to the customer’s situation.

Transition to Next-Gen SAP Ariba

Next-gen SAP Ariba represents a complete architectural transformation of the source-to-pay suite. This is not a simple upgrade but a fundamental modernization that moves procurement from reactive to proactive and from manual to autonomous processes.

Current Status

  • First release launched: February 2026 (2602 release) is now available
  • Beta testing completed: Customer beta programs concluded
  • Phased rollout approach: SAP is delivering capabilities through structured release waves rather than a single migration event
  • No forced migration: Organizations can transition on their own timeline without mandatory cutover dates

Key Transition Considerations

  • SAP BTP readiness: Next-gen Ariba requires SAP Business Technology Platform as the foundation, making BTP setup a prerequisite
  • Unified supplier master data: The new architecture consolidates supplier data across all modules, eliminating fragmentation between sourcing, contracts, buying, and invoicing
  • Identity and access management: Centralized user provisioning through SAP Cloud Identity Services becomes the single point of management
  • No new contracts required: Existing SAP Ariba customers can transition without signing new SKUs or licensing agreements
  • Custom field dependencies: Organizations should evaluate existing customizations and extensions for compatibility with the new BTP-based architecture

Release Readiness

Links below can be referred in general for the details of features during each quarterly release. Kindly note that Next-gen release details are not yet part of it.

  • Help for What's New in SAP Procurement and SAP Business Network | SAP Help Portal
  • What's New in SAP Procurement and SAP Business Network | SAP Help Portal

For monthly release features about SAP Ariba Invoicing (part of Next-gen SAP Ariba), kindly refer What's New in SAP Ariba Invoicing | SAP Help Portal.

 

Conclusion

Next-gen SAP Ariba provides a decisive shift in procurement - from process-driven execution to intelligent, autonomous decision-making. By combining AI, unified data, and seamless integration on SAP Business Technology Platform, it enables organizations to move beyond efficiency and unlock true strategic value.

While next-gen SAP Ariba drives intelligent procurement from within, SAP Business Network extends that intelligence across a global ecosystem - turning transactions into connected, data-driven value chains. The future of procurement is not just digital - it is intelligent, connected, and proactive.

 

Consolidated List of Resources

  • Brief overview of the solution - Next-Gen SAP Ariba | Smart AI Source-to-Pay Software
  • Quick demo - Video (8 minutes)
  • Next-Gen SAP Ariba | SAP Help Portal

  • Administration Center for SAP Ariba Solutions | SAP Help Portal

  • Administering Next-Gen SAP Integration Suite, Managed Gateway For Spend Management and SAP Business Network | SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Partner Portal | Next-gen SAP Ariba
  • Positioning next-gen SAP Ariba (1 Hour)
  • Transforming Procurement: Introduction to Next-gen SAP Ariba for Partners (Recording - 1 Hour)
  • Implementing Next-Gen SAP Ariba: Onboarding through Extensibility (Recording - 3 Hours)
  • Selling Next-Gen SAP Ariba: Guidance for Partners (Learning Hub)(By Mark Pickering, Dan, Prem)

entry.title: SAP AI: Beyond the Hype and Into the Real World
entry.content:
Three days in Excel hell. Twenty supplier bids. One AI agent that knows your data. This is the SAP AI story your vendor deck won't tell you — first in a series.

I've been on the digital transformation journey for more years than I care to admit. I truly believe that constant evolution is the only way to stay relevant in procurement, but lately, it feels like the word "transformation" has been completely hijacked by a single two-letter acronym: AI. Everywhere I turn, AI is the only topic in the room. It is being pitched as the ultimate cure-all, yet for most of us, the daily reality still involves wrestling with legacy systems and messy data.

After endless conversations about AI's impact today, where it is going, and how it fits into our daily lives, I've realized that what we need is not more noise, but a clear roadmap.

My intention with this series is to provide a solid understanding of where we are today, set some realistic expectations, and point out where the magic really happens when we get it right.

We will look at what SAP is doing broadly with Business AI, while zooming in specifically on the procurement space where the impact is most immediate. Each post in this series will focus on a specific piece of the puzzle or a real-world scenario, helping you not only navigate this AI transformation but ensuring you actually see tangible value at the end of it. And I promise: no marketing fluff and no buzzword bingo.

 

The Anatomy of Success: How the Pieces Actually Fit Together

Stop thinking about AI as a single feature you can switch on. It's a high-performance system built from three distinct, non-negotiable layers. Pull any one out and the whole thing fails to deliver value. Here's the blueprint:

 The three layers of the SAP AI stack. Remove any one and the system fails to deliver value.

Each layer has a clear job. BTP is where your code actually runs and your security lives. SAP Business Data Cloud is the part that prevents your AI from getting confused — it makes sure a "Supplier" in one system is the same thing as a "Vendor" in another. Joule is the conversational front door that takes your natural-language request and orchestrates the work across every back-end system. Everything else in this post depends on these three working together.

 

Four Flavors of SAP AI You'll Actually Encounter

Not all AI is created equal — and you'll meet at least four different flavors in any real SAP deployment. Each one solves a different problem and shows up in your workflow in a different way. Here's how to recognize them:

 Four flavors of SAP AI. Each one solves a different problem — and you'll meet all of them

 

Talk Is Cheap. Let's Look at a Real Scenario.

We've all heard the lofty promises of AI, but talk is notoriously cheap. Instead of another high-level theory, I want to show you a practical scenario that has already been built and is currently saving procurement experts hundreds of hours. This is not some futuristic concept waiting for a release date — it's how we're solving real-world complexity right now.

 The Bid Analysis Agent in action — what 80% of the heavy lifting looks like.

WHAT HAPPENS TODAY WITHOUT AI

You're stuck in Excel hell for three days. You're manually pivoting data, trying to run "what if" scenarios, and likely making a few coffee-fueled typos along the way. By the time you finally have an answer, the market data has already shifted and your analysis is effectively a historical document.

THE BASIC AI EXPERIENCE

You try to use a generic, public AI tool. You upload a spreadsheet and ask for an analysis. It gives you a shallow "cheapest is best" answer, hallucinates a few shipping routes that don't exist, and potentially leaks your sensitive pricing data to the public web. Fast, but dangerous — and completely lacking business context.

THE REALISTIC SAP AI FUTURE

You use a Bid Analysis Agent grounded in your actual Ariba data. You ask Joule to show the award scenario that minimizes risk if fuel prices spike. The agent evaluates the trade-offs, highlights bid outliers, and suggests a data-driven strategy in seconds by factoring in unit prices, shipping, and risk data. You're still the decision-maker — but the AI has handled 80% of the analytical heavy lifting for you.


Straight Talk: Paying the Data Tax

AI in 2026 is not about replacing the procurement professional. It is about capacity. It's about getting that 20% to 30% of your week back from repetitive, manual work.

But let's be real about the Data Tax. I'm not suggesting every organization is starting from scratch. In fact, most customers I talk to have done a great job with their supplier and master data. The real mess usually hides elsewhere: in the offline spreadsheets, the unstructured sourcing history, and the fragmented procurement processes that haven't quite made it into the system yet.

 

Two data realities. One of them turns your AI into a confident liar.

Good data is structured, centralized, and consistent. It means having one version of the truth, so that when you ask an AI for a risk assessment, it isn't hallucinating because it found three different versions of the same contract.

Bad data is the fragmented manual sprawl — the tribal knowledge and the manual workarounds that live outside your digital core.

When you try to use AI with bad data, the implications are simple: the AI becomes "confidently wrong." It will give you an answer, but that answer will be based on a fragment of reality. Paying the Data Tax is not a one-time activity or a weekend cleanup. It's an ongoing evolution in how you store and leverage your information — a commitment to continuous improvement where your AI outputs steadily improve as your data strategy matures.

You simply cannot ignore the data layer of the architecture and expect the interaction layer to give you accurate results.

What's Next

Moving forward, this series will dive deep into the specific AI scenarios and features that drive actual savings in both time and money. I'll be sharing not just what we're building at SAP, but also the real-world best practices I'm seeing from other leading organizations that are successfully closing the "execution gap." There's a wealth of peer knowledge out there that can benefit us all.

I have specific use cases planned, but I'm always interested in your perspective. If there's a particular challenge you're facing or a piece of the puzzle you want to see solved, let me know what you'd like me to cover in future chapters.

entry.title: Meet The Expert: Next-gen SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Contracts - Live Demo
entry.content:
We are excited to share details regarding an upcoming Live Demo on Next-gen SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Contracts. 

Aimed at existing SAP Ariba customers, this demo will provide a hands-on look at what’s new, what’s changing, and how to prepare for the transition. Through live product demos and real-world use cases, you’ll see exactly how Next-gen SAP Ariba differs from the classic experience, and the business value it can unlock.

We are beyond offering a feature pack or a user interface refresh.  We are bringing you a complete reengineering of SAP Ariba as an AI native platform - think fully microservices-based model that offers greater scalability and faster, lower impact innovation.  AI takes on the burden of manual processes and frees your procurement team to focus on strategic, high value business decisions. 

Join the Upcoming Session 

Date: Wednesday, July 8, 2026 

Topic: MTE - Next-gen SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Contracts

Time: 9:00 am – 9:30 am CDT

Language: English

Register for the event through the Spend Management Value Map Events and access the complete document on this topic here. 

To access Value Map Groups, you must login to SAP Community with an S-user ID or a Universal ID. 

Note: To provision a Universal ID, please follow these steps:  

(1) Visit the SAP Universal ID website 

(2) sign up for an account using your company email, and 

(3) link your existing S-user ID to your new Universal ID. 

 This session is relevant for our existing SAP Ariba customer team members: 

  • Developer 
  • Business User 
  • Administrator 
  • Architect

It will provide practical insights into the roadmap, architecture, and what steps you should start considering now. 

This is just the beginning. More events will continue to be populated under the Spend Management Value Map Events, so stay tuned and check back regularly to see what’s coming next.

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps   

Beyond this topic, SAP Enterprise Support value maps on SAP Community provides guidance, recommended actions, and supporting resources to help you decide what to focus on next—all included in your SAP cloud subscription.   

👉Watch short demo video, then head over to  our page  explore our range of topics and request a call  with a topic expert for guidance tailored to your goals.    

About the author:

Ernie Cosper is a Topic Expert for SAP Enterprise Support value maps, specializing in Spend Management. With over a decade at SAP and prior to this another decade in consulting and implementations, she brings deep experience from a range of customer-facing and managerial roles. Ernie enjoys working with customers, partners and peers to drive value and forge success from the foundation of SAP Enterprise Support.

entry.title: Ariba Next Gen: Unlocking Flexibility with Extensibility Hub
entry.content:
As organizations evolve, so do their business requirements. Standard application capabilities often need to be enhanced to meet specific operational needs. This is where the Extensibility Hub in Ariba Next Gen plays a crucial role—empowering administrators to customize and extend application functionality with ease.

🔍What is Extensibility Hub:  A shared service framework that allows administrators to create and manage custom extensions. These extensions can be published across multiple Ariba modules, including: Sourcing, Contracts, Buying, invoicing etc.

- Unlimited Custom Fields No restriction on the number of custom fields you can create
- Cross-Application Reusability Reuse fields across multiple modules effortlessly            
- Expression Support Build business logic without affecting standard functionality
- Synchronization (Sync) Align and reuse custom fields across project
- Version History Tracking Easily track project changes over time
- Agent Support Extend functionality further with agents
- Business Usage Extend fields across modules and expose them to CIG
- Custom field should get auto replicated to S4 Hana public cloud

 

⚠️Before you start, keep these key points in mind:

- Expression cannot be tested directly in Extensibility Hub
- Must be validated via UI adoption and navigation
-️ Technical Naming Rules Field technical names must NOT contain spaces
- Publishing Time Publishing custom fields takes approximately 7–8 minutes

🛠️ How to Configure follow these simple steps to get started:

Navigate to Extensibility Hub:  Admin → Global Settings → Extensibility (this will land admin user to the extensibility hub)

 

Create an Extensibility Project, Start a new project

Project is a collection of entities and extensions (both regular and customized) created for an application. It serves as the container for managing and organizing extensions, allowing grouping related entities together for easier deployment

project is a collection of entities and extensions (both regular and customized) created for an application. It serves as the container for managing and organizing extensions, allowing grouping related entities together for easier deployment

Define Field Placement, create field and add expression under view content

choose header or line item to add the field

 technical name should be without space

 Write expression under view content ✅ Align logic with business needs

Publish Custom Fields

🚀Publish your fields ⏱️ Allow ~7–8 minutes for deployment (Click publish to make fields available to Adopt on UI)

 

Adopt Fields in UI

- Enable via UI adoption process

- Validate through navigation before making visible

Click on Adopt UI to make field available on UI

 
 
 

Click on navigation to test the expression before you make fields available for end users

🏁Final Thoughts

The Extensibility Hub in Ariba Next Gen provides a powerful yet controlled way to enhance applications based on business needs. By using it effectively, organizations can achieve the perfect balance between Standardization and Customization

👉Resulting in both stability and agility across business processes.
